flags.concern: Timeline summary context: the plan assumes backend timeline config already carries shot names, but `pinnedShotGroups` only stores `shotId`/`trackId`/`clipIds`/`mode`, so `describeTimeline()` cannot emit shot names without an additional shot lookup or a widened input contract.
flags.evidence: FLAG-001 through FLAG-003 indicate the original plan was aimed at the wrong root cause in one area: the UI click-to-select affordance already exists, so adding a new `TimelineCanvas` callback would duplicate working code instead of fixing the missing shot-context pipeline. The revised plan removes that stale frontend scope, treats shot names as external data that must be loaded explicitly on the backend, and centralizes same-shot resolution so `runAgentLoop`, `create_task`, `set_lora`, and `duplicate_generation` all use the same shot-context rules rather than patching only `duplicate_generation`.

flags.concern: Selected-clip payload plumbing: Recurring debt: the plan updates some payload types but misses the stored attachment path, so `shot_id`/`shot_name` would be dropped by AgentChat send mapping and by attachment persistence/recovery across turns.
flags.evidence: FLAG-001 through FLAG-003 indicate the original plan was aimed at the wrong root cause in one area: the UI click-to-select affordance already exists, so adding a new `TimelineCanvas` callback would duplicate working code instead of fixing the missing shot-context pipeline. The revised plan removes that stale frontend scope, treats shot names as external data that must be loaded explicitly on the backend, and centralizes same-shot resolution so `runAgentLoop`, `create_task`, `set_lora`, and `duplicate_generation` all use the same shot-context rules rather than patching only `duplicate_generation`.

flags.concern: Same-shot semantics: the plan only applies direct `shot_id` to `duplicate_generation`, but shot resolution for travel defaults, active references, task shot reuse, and video-travel LoRAs still goes through `findShotForGenerations()`, so the feature remains only partially first-class.

flags.concern: Frontend shot lookup source: the revised plan still says `useSelectedMediaClips()` can read `pinnedShotGroups` from `resolvedConfig`, but that property does not exist on `ResolvedTimelineConfig`, so the step still points at the wrong state source.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked step 3 against the actual `useSelectedMediaClips()` inputs in [useSelectedMediaClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). That hook currently reads only `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig` from editor context, so the plan's literal instruction to join `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups` with `useShots()` data will not compile unless it first switches to `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` or another real source of pinned groups.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the caller contract of `useSelectedMediaClips()` via [useTimelineEditorData](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). This hook's caller currently provides `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig`, not a resolved config object with `pinnedShotGroups`, so the plan still does not handle the actual arguments passed at that call site and needs to pull pinned groups from editor `data` instead.
